description: User is asking how to open the mod menu, config settings, or the graphical user interface.
answer: To open the config GUI, use `/na`

description: User wants to know how to resize, scale, or change the size of HUD elements on their screen.
answer: Click and hold the HUD element and use your scroll wheel to scale it.

description: User is asking for the best settings, median values, or delay ranges for the auto-terminal feature.
answer:
The delay is ping based so the median value should be around 150 - ping. If this result is negative then you probably can use 0 delay!
Random delay is based on a Gaussian Distribution so your min and max should usually be around 80% - 120% of the calculated median respectively.

For example: if you have 100 ping it will be 150 - 100 = 50. So your delay range should be 40 - 60ms.

description: User is asking how to get a custom name, change their player size, or how to donate for cosmetics.
answer: You can get custom names and player sizes by donating to the mod. Ping <@601764042704683040> regarding how to donate.

description: User reports that their game crashed, closed unexpectedly, or they need help with logs/crash reports.
answer: If you want help, first you need to provide sufficient information so others can understand the problem. Upload relevant crash reports, `latest.log`, or `hs_err_pid<n>.log` files straight into Discord or to https://mclo.gs/ and send the result in a place like #general with context about your issue — someone will probably help you.

description: User is asking if the mod is safe, if they will get banned, or if it is 'spec-safe'.
answer: Features in the cheat version of NA are made to be spec-safe and watchdog-safe, so probably not. But be sure to think about the settings you use. Also understand that every mod has some inherent risk involved, no matter if it's a cheat or not.

description: User is asking how to download beta versions, dev builds, or how to update the mod via GitHub.
answer:
GitHub Actions are like betas of the mod you can download between releases. To get one:
1. Go to the NA GitHub
2. Log into GitHub
3. Go to the **Actions** tab at the top
4. Click the latest action applicable to your version (e.g. `main` branch or `1.21.11` branch)
5. Download the zip file at the bottom and extract it
6. Use the respective `.jar` mod in the folder relevant to you (cheat or legit)

description: User is checking the status of the mod's API, backend, or wondering why certain features are slow/offline.
